content.problemStatement:
Given a string sss which consists of lowercase latin letters, return the length of the **longest palindrome** that can be built with those letters.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains the string `s`.


Output Format

Output on a single line, the length of the longest palindrome that can be format using the letters of `s`.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string s consisting only of lowercase English letters (1 ≤ |s| ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the maximum possible length of a palindrome that can be built using all or some of the characters of s.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For "bbd" we can use both b's (2) and place the single d in the centre, giving length 3. For "aabdcd" the optimal palindrome uses five characters, e.g., "adbda".
A single character itself forms a palindrome of length 1.
All three characters appear once; only one of them can be placed in the centre, so the longest palindrome length is 1.

solution.approach: For each test case count how many times each of the 26 letters appears. Every pair of equal letters can be placed symmetrically in the palindrome, contributing 2 to the length. After using all possible pairs, if there is at least one character with an odd remaining count we can place exactly one of them in the centre, adding 1. Thus the answer is sum_{c}(freq[c] / 2) * 2 plus (any odd ? 1 : 0). This greedy choice is optimal because any palindrome can use at most the even part of each character and at most one odd character as the centre.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|s|) per test case, i.e. O(T·|s|) overall (≤ 1000 operations).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a fixed array of 26 integers is used.
